rationale: Both reach the correct 'duplicate of #4808' disposition, but the actual thread explicitly points to #4809's comment for 'additional details' — Y correctly surfaces #4809 as the tracking issue coordinating the five call-site fixes, matching that reference, while X never mentions #4809 at all. X also asserts as fact in its posted answer that the fix 'is already fixed on main' despite its own closing caveat admitting merge-state was never verified via gh, which is an overconfident claim risk; Y instead explicitly checks and flags that #4808 shows closed/unmerged, transparently separating what it could verify (rebase-branch state) from what it couldn't (upstream main), and raises a concrete follow-up (test coverage gap) that engages the reporter's real end-to-end concern rather than just formulaically restating fix/verify/prevent boilerplate.
